revision.text:
'''善知鳥神社'''（うとうじんじゃ）は、[[青森県]][[青森市]]安方にある[[神社]]である。社格は旧[[県社]]。
==祭神== *[[宗像三神]]
==歴史== この神社の創建年代等については不詳であるが、[[允恭天皇]]の代に善知鳥中納言安方という者によって創建されたと伝えられる。
==所在地== *青森県青森市安方2-7-18
==外部リンク== *[http://www.actv.ne.jp/~utou/ 善知鳥神社サイト]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うとうしんしや}} [[Category:青森市]] [[Category:青森県の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''善知鳥神社'''（うとうじんじゃ）は、[[青森県]][[青森市]]安方にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]。
== 祭神 == 海の神、航海安全の神として知られる市杵島姫命・多岐津姫命・多紀理姫命の[[宗像三女神]]を主祭神として祀る。他に祭神として倉稲魂命、宮比命、猿田彦命、海津見大神が祀られている。
== 概要 == [[允恭天皇]]の時代に[[善知鳥安方|善知鳥中納言安方]]という者が勅勘を受けて外ヶ浜に蟄居していた時に高倉明神の霊夢に感じて干潟に小さな祠を建設し、宗像三神を祀ったのが神社の起こりと伝わる。安方が亡くなると、見慣れない一番の鳥が小祠のほとりに飛んできて雄はウトウと鳴き、雌はヤスカタと鳴くので、人々はこの鳥を安方の化身として恐れ敬ったが、ある日猟師が誤ってこの雄鳥を狙撃してしまい、以後雄鳥によって田畑が荒らされた。狙撃した猟師も変死したため、祟りを恐れた同郷の人々は雄鳥を丁重に弔った。
その後の流れは不明であるが、[[坂上田村麻呂]]の時代には同祠は荒廃していたため、[[大同]]年間に田村麻呂により社殿が造営されて再興された。[[建仁]]から[[天正]]期（[[1201年]]～[[1592年]]）の間は同地を支配した領主の[[南部氏]]により厚く崇敬されて庇護され、南部氏の騒動で同地が[[江戸時代]]に[[津軽氏]]の支配地になっても、同氏により手厚い庇護を受けた。[[明治時代]]に入り、[[1873年]]に[[郷社]]、[[1876年]]に[[県社]]に昇格。[[1907年]]7月に[[稲荷神社]]、同年9月に[[海津見神社]]を合祀し、現在に至る。
== 関連項目 == *[[ウトウ]]（善知鳥／海鳥） *[[謡曲]]『[[善知鳥]]』 *[[棟方志功]] 版画家・画家。幼少期をこの神社の近くで過ごした。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.actv.ne.jp/~utou/ 善知鳥神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm 善知鳥神社]（青森県神社庁）

その後の流れは不明であるが、[[坂上田村麻呂]]の時代には同祠は荒廃していたため、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]年間に田村麻呂により社殿が造営されて再興された。[[建仁]]から[[天正]]期（[[1201年]]～[[1592年]]）の間は同地を支配した領主の[[南部氏]]により厚く崇敬されて庇護され、南部氏の騒動で同地が[[江戸時代]]に[[津軽氏]]の支配地になっても、同氏により手厚い庇護を受けた。[[明治時代]]に入り、[[1873年]]に[[郷社]]、[[1876年]]に[[県社]]に昇格。[[1907年]]7月に[[稲荷神社]]、同年9月に[[海津見神社]]を合祀し、現在に至る。

== 祭神 == 海の神、航海安全の神として知られる市杵島姫命・多岐津姫命・多紀理姫命の[[宗像三女神]]を主祭神として祀る。他に祭神として倉稲魂命、宮比命、猿田彦命、海津見大神が祀られている。
== 概要 == [[允恭天皇]]の時代に[[善知鳥安方|善知鳥中納言安方]]という者が勅勘を受けて外ヶ浜に蟄居していた時に高倉明神の霊夢に感じて干潟に小さな祠を建設し、宗像三神を祀ったのが神社の起こりと伝わる。安方が亡くなると、見慣れない一番の鳥が小祠のほとりに飛んできて雄はウトウと鳴き、雌はヤスカタと鳴くので、人々はこの鳥を安方の化身として恐れ敬ったが、ある日猟師が誤ってこの雄鳥を狙撃してしまい、以後雄鳥によって田畑が荒らされた。狙撃した猟師も変死したため、祟りを恐れた同郷の人々は雄鳥を丁重に弔った。
その後の流れは不明であるが、[[坂上田村麻呂]]の時代には同祠は荒廃していたため、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]年間に田村麻呂により社殿が造営されて再興された。[[建仁]]から[[天正]]期（[[1201年]]～[[1592年]]）の間は同地を支配した領主の[[南部氏]]により厚く崇敬されて庇護され、南部氏の騒動で同地が[[江戸時代]]に[[津軽氏]]の支配地になっても、同氏により手厚い庇護を受けた。[[明治時代]]に入り、[[1873年]]に[[郷社]]、[[1876年]]に[[県社]]に昇格。[[1907年]]7月に[[稲荷神社]]、同年9月に[[海津見神社]]を合祀し、現在に至る。
[[2020年]]12月に[[バーチャルYouTuber]]の[[青森りんこ]]とコラボレーションした[[絵馬]]の授与を始めた<ref>{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ」|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[ウトウ]]（善知鳥／海鳥） *[[謡曲]]『[[善知鳥]]』 *[[棟方志功]] 版画家・画家。幼少期をこの神社の近くで過ごした。
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat}} *[http://www.actv.ne.jp/~utou/ 善知鳥神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm 善知鳥神社]（青森県神社庁） *[https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ 公式ブログ 参拝のすゝめ]
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:うとうしんしや}} [[Category:青森県の神社]] [[Category:青森県の旧県社]] [[Category:青森市の歴史]] [[Category:青森市の建築物]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年8月}} {{神社 |名称 = 善知鳥神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = [[青森県]][[青森市]]安方2-7-18 |位置 = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 宗像三女神 |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=善知鳥神社|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''善知鳥神社'''（うとうじんじゃ）は、[[青森県]][[青森市]]安方にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == 海の神、航海安全の神として知られる市杵島姫命・多岐津姫命・多紀理姫命の[[宗像三女神]]を主祭神として祀る。他に祭神として倉稲魂命、宮比命、猿田彦命、海津見大神が祀られている。 == 概要 == [[允恭天皇]]の時代に[[善知鳥安方|善知鳥中納言安方]]という者が勅勘を受けて外ヶ浜に蟄居していた時に高倉明神の霊夢に感じて干潟に小さな祠を建設し、宗像三神を祀ったのが神社の起こりと伝わる。安方が亡くなると、見慣れない一番の鳥が小祠のほとりに飛んできて雄はウトウと鳴き、雌はヤスカタと鳴くので、人々はこの鳥を安方の化身として恐れ敬ったが、ある日猟師が誤ってこの雄鳥を狙撃してしまい、以後雄鳥によって田畑が荒らされた。狙撃した猟師も変死したため、祟りを恐れた同郷の人々は雄鳥を丁重に弔った。 その後の流れは不明であるが、[[坂上田村麻呂]]の時代には同祠は荒廃していたため、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]年間に田村麻呂により社殿が造営されて再興された。[[建仁]]から[[天正]]期（[[1201年]]～[[1592年]]）の間は同地を支配した領主の[[南部氏]]により厚く崇敬されて庇護され、南部氏の騒動で同地が[[江戸時代]]に[[津軽氏]]の支配地になっても、同氏により手厚い庇護を受けた。[[明治時代]]に入り、[[1873年]]に[[郷社]]、[[1876年]]に[[県社]]に昇格。[[1907年]]7月に[[稲荷神社]]、同年9月に[[海津見神社]]を合祀し、現在に至る。 [[2020年]]12月に[[バーチャルYouTuber]]の[[青森りんこ]]とコラボレーションした[[絵馬]]の授与を始めた{{Cite web|和書|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ」|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}}。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[ウトウ]]（善知鳥／海鳥） *[[謡曲]]『[[善知鳥]]』 *[[棟方志功]]　版画家・画家。幼少期をこの神社の近くで過ごした。 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat}} *[http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ 善知鳥神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm 善知鳥神社]（青森県神社庁） *[https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ 公式ブログ 参拝のすゝめ] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うとうしんしや}} [[Category:青森県の神社]] [[Category:青森県の旧県社]] [[Category:青森市の歴史]] [[Category:青森市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox shrine | name = Utō Shrine | image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden (worship hall) | coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | address = 2-7-18 Anzokata, Aomori City, Aomori Prefecture, Japan | deities = {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festivals = {{nihongo|September 15|9月15日}} | map = Japan Aomori }} '''Utō Shrine''' ({{nihongo|善知鳥神社|うとうじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, Aomori Prefecture, Japan. It was previously classified as a [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the prewar [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}—Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto, Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto, and Tagirihime-no-Mikoto—deities of the sea and maritime safety. Additionally, the shrine honors deities such as {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命}}, and Kaijin ({{nihongo|Watatsumi Ōkami|海津見大神}}). == Overview == The origins of the shrine date back to the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]] when a noble named {{nihongo|Utō Chūnagon Yasukata|善知鳥中納言安方}} was exiled to the outer shores ({{nihongo|Sotogahama|外ヶ浜}}). Inspired by a divine revelation from Takakura Myōjin, Yasukata constructed a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the Munakata Three Goddesses. After his death, a strange bird appeared near the shrine; its male cried "Utō," and its female cried "Yasukata," leading locals to revere the bird as a manifestation of Yasukata. However, when a hunter mistakenly killed the male bird, misfortune followed, prompting the villagers to conduct proper rites for the bird. In later years, the shrine fell into disrepair. During the {{nihongo|Daidō era|大同}}, {{nihongo|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|坂上田村麻呂}} restored the shrine. From the {{nihongo|Kenin|建仁}} to {{nihongo|Tenshō|天正}} eras (1201–1592), the {{nihongo|Nanbu clan|南部氏}} supported the shrine, followed by the {{nihongo|Tsugaru clan|津軽氏}} during the Edo period. In the Meiji era, the shrine was elevated to {{nihongo|Gōsha|郷社}} in 1873 and to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, the shrine absorbed the nearby {{nihongo|Inari Shrine|稲荷神社}} and {{nihongo|Kaijin Shrine|海津見神社}}. In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]]

{{Infobox shrine | name = Utō Shrine | image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden (worship hall) | coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | address = 2-7-18 Anzokata, Aomori City, Aomori Prefecture, Japan | deities = {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festivals = {{nihongo|September 15|9月15日}} | map = Japan Aomori }} '''Utō Shrine''' ({{nihongo|善知鳥神社|うとうじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, Aomori Prefecture, Japan. It was previously classified as a [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the prewar [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}—Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto, Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto, and Tagirihime-no-Mikoto—deities of the sea and maritime safety. Additionally, the shrine honors deities such as {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命}}, and Kaijin ({{nihongo|Watatsumi Ōkami|海津見大神}}). == Overview == The origins of the shrine date back to the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]] when a noble named {{nihongo|Utō Chūnagon Yasukata|善知鳥中納言安方}} was exiled to the outer shores ({{nihongo|Sotogahama|外ヶ浜}}). Inspired by a divine revelation from Takakura Myōjin, Yasukata constructed a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the Munakata Three Goddesses. After his death, a strange bird appeared near the shrine; its male cried "Utō," and its female cried "Yasukata," leading locals to revere the bird as a manifestation of Yasukata. However, when a hunter mistakenly killed the male bird, misfortune followed, prompting the villagers to conduct proper rites for the bird. In later years, the shrine fell into disrepair. During the {{nihongo|Daidō era|大同}}, {{nihongo|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|坂上田村麻呂}} restored the shrine. From the {{nihongo|Kenin|建仁}} to {{nihongo|Tenshō|天正}} eras (1201–1592), the {{nihongo|Nanbu clan|南部氏}} supported the shrine, followed by the {{nihongo|Tsugaru clan|津軽氏}} during the Edo period. In the Meiji era, the shrine was elevated to {{nihongo|Gōsha|郷社}} in 1873 and to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, the shrine absorbed the nearby {{nihongo|Inari Shrine|稲荷神社}} and {{nihongo|Kaijin Shrine|海津見神社}}. In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]]

Torii and Haiden (worship hall) | coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | address = 2-7-18 Anzokata, Aomori City, Aomori Prefecture, Japan | deities = {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festivals = {{nihongo|September 15|9月15日}} | map = Japan Aomori }} '''Utō Shrine''' ({{nihongo|善知鳥神社|うとうじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, Aomori Prefecture, Japan. {{Infobox shrine | name = Utō Shrine | image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]

In 1907, the shrine absorbed the nearby {{nihongo|Inari Shrine|稲荷神社}} and {{nihongo|Kaijin Shrine|海津見神社}}. In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]

{{Infobox shrine | name = Utō Shrine | image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden (worship hall) | coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 2-7-18 Anzokata, Aomori City, Aomori Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festivals = {{nihongo|September 15|9月15日}} | map = Japan Aomori }} '''Utō Shrine''' ({{nihongo|善知鳥神社|うとうじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, Aomori Prefecture, Japan. It was previously classified as a [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the prewar [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}—Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto, Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto, and Tagirihime-no-Mikoto—deities of the sea and maritime safety. Additionally, the shrine honors deities such as {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命}}, and Kaijin ({{nihongo|Watatsumi Ōkami|海津見大神}}). == Overview == The origins of the shrine date back to the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]] when a noble named {{nihongo|Utō Chūnagon Yasukata|善知鳥中納言安方}} was exiled to the outer shores ({{nihongo|Sotogahama|外ヶ浜}}). Inspired by a divine revelation from Takakura Myōjin, Yasukata constructed a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the Munakata Three Goddesses. After his death, a strange bird appeared near the shrine; its male cried "Utō," and its female cried "Yasukata," leading locals to revere the bird as a manifestation of Yasukata. However, when a hunter mistakenly killed the male bird, misfortune followed, prompting the villagers to conduct proper rites for the bird. In later years, the shrine fell into disrepair. During the {{nihongo|Daidō era|大同}}, {{nihongo|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|坂上田村麻呂}} restored the shrine. From the {{nihongo|Kenin|建仁}} to {{nihongo|Tenshō|天正}} eras (1201–1592), the {{nihongo|Nanbu clan|南部氏}} supported the shrine, followed by the {{nihongo|Tsugaru clan|津軽氏}} during the Edo period. In the Meiji era, the shrine was elevated to {{nihongo|Gōsha|郷社}} in 1873 and to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, the shrine absorbed the nearby {{nihongo|Inari Shrine|稲荷神社}} and {{nihongo|Kaijin Shrine|海津見神社}}. In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]

{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Shrine |Name = Uto Shrine |Image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternate Names = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyo|ja|允恭天皇}}, a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the Munakata Sanjojin. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, it incorporated {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]]

However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, it incorporated {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]]

Torii and Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternate Names = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyo|ja|允恭天皇}}, a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the Munakata Sanjojin. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, it incorporated {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Utō Shrine | image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden (worship hall) | coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 2-7-18 Anzokata, Aomori City, Aomori Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festivals = {{nihongo|September 15|9月15日}} | map = Japan Aomori }} '''Utō Shrine''' ({{nihongo|善知鳥神社|うとうじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, Aomori Prefecture, Japan. It was previously classified as a [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the prewar [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}—Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto, Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto, and Tagirihime-no-Mikoto—deities of the sea and maritime safety. Additionally, the shrine honors deities such as {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命}}, and Kaijin ({{nihongo|Watatsumi Ōkami|海津見大神}}). == Overview == The origins of the shrine date back to the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]] when a noble named {{nihongo|Utō Chūnagon Yasukata|善知鳥中納言安方}} was exiled to the outer shores ({{nihongo|Sotogahama|外ヶ浜}}). Inspired by a divine revelation from Takakura Myōjin, Yasukata constructed a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the Munakata Three Goddesses. After his death, a strange bird appeared near the shrine; its male cried "Utō," and its female cried "Yasukata," leading locals to revere the bird as a manifestation of Yasukata. However, when a hunter mistakenly killed the male bird, misfortune followed, prompting the villagers to conduct proper rites for the bird. In later years, the shrine fell into disrepair. During the {{nihongo|Daidō era|大同}}, {{nihongo|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|坂上田村麻呂}} restored the shrine. From the {{nihongo|Kenin|建仁}} to {{nihongo|Tenshō|天正}} eras (1201–1592), the {{nihongo|Nanbu clan|南部氏}} supported the shrine, followed by the {{nihongo|Tsugaru clan|津軽氏}} during the Edo period. In the Meiji era, the shrine was elevated to {{nihongo|Gōsha|郷社}} in 1873 and to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, the shrine absorbed the nearby {{nihongo|Inari Shrine|稲荷神社}} and {{nihongo|Kaijin Shrine|海津見神社}}. In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Utō Shrine | image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden (worship hall) | coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 2-7-18 Anzokata, Aomori City, Aomori Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festivals = {{nihongo|September 15|9月15日}} | map = Japan Aomori }} '''Utō Shrine''' ({{nihongo|善知鳥神社|うとうじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, Aomori Prefecture, Japan. It was previously classified as a [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the prewar [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}—Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto, Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto, and Tagirihime-no-Mikoto—deities of the sea and maritime safety. Additionally, the shrine honors deities such as {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命}}, and Kaijin ({{nihongo|Watatsumi Ōkami|海津見大神}}). == Overview == The origins of the shrine date back to the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]] when a noble named {{nihongo|Utō Chūnagon Yasukata|善知鳥中納言安方}} was exiled to the outer shores ({{nihongo|Sotogahama|外ヶ浜}}). Inspired by a divine revelation from Takakura Myōjin, Yasukata constructed a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the Munakata Three Goddesses. After his death, a strange bird appeared near the shrine; its male cried "Utō," and its female cried "Yasukata," leading locals to revere the bird as a manifestation of Yasukata. However, when a hunter mistakenly killed the male bird, misfortune followed, prompting the villagers to conduct proper rites for the bird. In later years, the shrine fell into disrepair. During the {{nihongo|Daidō era|大同}}, {{nihongo|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|坂上田村麻呂}} restored the shrine. From the {{nihongo|Kenin|建仁}} to {{nihongo|Tenshō|天正}} eras (1201–1592), the {{nihongo|Nanbu clan|南部氏}} supported the shrine, followed by the {{nihongo|Tsugaru clan|津軽氏}} during the Edo period. In the Meiji era, the shrine was elevated to {{nihongo|Gōsha|郷社}} in 1873 and to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, the shrine absorbed the nearby {{nihongo|Inari Shrine|稲荷神社}} and {{nihongo|Kaijin Shrine|海津見神社}}. In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]][[ja:善知鳥神社]]

In the Meiji era, the shrine was elevated to {{nihongo|Gōsha|郷社}} in 1873 and to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, the shrine absorbed the nearby {{nihongo|Inari Shrine|稲荷神社}} and {{nihongo|Kaijin Shrine|海津見神社}}. In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]]

{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Shrine |Name = Uto Shrine |image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternate Names = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyo|ja|允恭天皇}}, a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the Munakata Sanjojin. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, it incorporated {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]]

In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:善知鳥神社]]

{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Shrine |Name = Uto Shrine |image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternate Names = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyo|ja|允恭天皇}}, a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the Munakata Sanjojin. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, it incorporated {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Shrine |Name = Uto Shrine |image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternate Names = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyo|ja|允恭天皇}}, a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now {{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Shrine |Name = Uto Shrine |image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternate Names = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyo|ja|允恭天皇}}, a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Utō Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Original pages]] to [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Utō Shrine | image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden (worship hall) | coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 2-7-18 Anzokata, Aomori City, Aomori Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festivals = {{nihongo|September 15|9月15日}} | map = Japan Aomori }} '''Utō Shrine''' ({{nihongo|善知鳥神社|うとうじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, Aomori Prefecture, Japan. It was previously classified as a [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the prewar [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}—Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto, Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto, and Tagirihime-no-Mikoto—deities of the sea and maritime safety. Additionally, the shrine honors deities such as {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命}}, and Kaijin ({{nihongo|Watatsumi Ōkami|海津見大神}}). == Overview == The origins of the shrine date back to the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]] when a noble named {{nihongo|Utō Chūnagon Yasukata|善知鳥中納言安方}} was exiled to the outer shores ({{nihongo|Sotogahama|外ヶ浜}}). Inspired by a divine revelation from Takakura Myōjin, Yasukata constructed a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the Munakata Three Goddesses. After his death, a strange bird appeared near the shrine; its male cried "Utō," and its female cried "Yasukata," leading locals to revere the bird as a manifestation of Yasukata. However, when a hunter mistakenly killed the male bird, misfortune followed, prompting the villagers to conduct proper rites for the bird. In later years, the shrine fell into disrepair. During the {{nihongo|Daidō era|大同}}, {{nihongo|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|坂上田村麻呂}} restored the shrine. From the {{nihongo|Kenin|建仁}} to {{nihongo|Tenshō|天正}} eras (1201–1592), the {{nihongo|Nanbu clan|南部氏}} supported the shrine, followed by the {{nihongo|Tsugaru clan|津軽氏}} during the Edo period. In the Meiji era, the shrine was elevated to {{nihongo|Gōsha|郷社}} in 1873 and to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, the shrine absorbed the nearby {{nihongo|Inari Shrine|稲荷神社}} and {{nihongo|Kaijin Shrine|海津見神社}}. In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:善知鳥神社]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Shrine |Name = Uto Shrine |image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternate Names = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyo|ja|允恭天皇}}, a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Utō Shrine | image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden (worship hall) | coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 2-7-18 Anzokata, Aomori City, Aomori Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festivals = {{nihongo|September 15|9月15日}} | map = Japan Aomori }} '''Utō Shrine''' ({{nihongo|善知鳥神社|うとうじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, Aomori Prefecture, Japan. It was previously classified as a [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the prewar [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}—Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto, Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto, and Tagirihime-no-Mikoto—deities of the sea and maritime safety. Additionally, the shrine honors deities such as {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命}}, and Kaijin ({{nihongo|Watatsumi Ōkami|海津見大神}}). == Overview == The origins of the shrine date back to the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]] when a noble named {{nihongo|Utō Chūnagon Yasukata|善知鳥中納言安方}} was exiled to the outer shores ({{nihongo|Sotogahama|外ヶ浜}}). Inspired by a divine revelation from Takakura Myōjin, Yasukata constructed a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the Munakata Three Goddesses. After his death, a strange bird appeared near the shrine; its male cried "Utō," and its female cried "Yasukata," leading locals to revere the bird as a manifestation of Yasukata. However, when a hunter mistakenly killed the male bird, misfortune followed, prompting the villagers to conduct proper rites for the bird. In later years, the shrine fell into disrepair. During the {{nihongo|Daidō era|大同}}, {{nihongo|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|坂上田村麻呂}} restored the shrine. From the {{nihongo|Kenin|建仁}} to {{nihongo|Tenshō|天正}} eras (1201–1592), the {{nihongo|Nanbu clan|南部氏}} supported the shrine, followed by the {{nihongo|Tsugaru clan|津軽氏}} during the Edo period. In the Meiji era, the shrine was elevated to {{nihongo|Gōsha|郷社}} in 1873 and to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, the shrine absorbed the nearby {{nihongo|Inari Shrine|稲荷神社}} and {{nihongo|Kaijin Shrine|海津見神社}}. In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]]

In 1907, the shrine absorbed the nearby {{nihongo|Inari Shrine|稲荷神社}} and {{nihongo|Kaijin Shrine|海津見神社}}. In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]]

In 1907, the shrine absorbed the nearby {{nihongo|Inari Shrine|稲荷神社}} and {{nihongo|Kaijin Shrine|海津見神社}}. In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]]

{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Shrine |Name = Uto Shrine |image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |Alternate Names = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市}}, Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyo|ja|允恭天皇}}, a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Utō Shrine | image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden (worship hall) | coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = 2-7-18 Anzokata, Aomori City, Aomori Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} | type = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festivals = {{nihongo|September 15|9月15日}} | map = Japan Aomori }} '''Utō Shrine''' ({{nihongo|善知鳥神社|うとうじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, Aomori Prefecture, Japan. It was previously classified as a [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the prewar [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine primarily enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}}—Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto, Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto, and Tagirihime-no-Mikoto—deities of the sea and maritime safety. Additionally, the shrine honors deities such as {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}}, {{ill|Miyabime|ja|宮比命}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命}}, and Kaijin ({{nihongo|Watatsumi Ōkami|海津見大神}}). == Overview == The origins of the shrine date back to the reign of [[Emperor Ingyō]] when a noble named {{nihongo|Utō Chūnagon Yasukata|善知鳥中納言安方}} was exiled to the outer shores ({{nihongo|Sotogahama|外ヶ浜}}). Inspired by a divine revelation from Takakura Myōjin, Yasukata constructed a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the Munakata Three Goddesses. After his death, a strange bird appeared near the shrine; its male cried "Utō," and its female cried "Yasukata," leading locals to revere the bird as a manifestation of Yasukata. However, when a hunter mistakenly killed the male bird, misfortune followed, prompting the villagers to conduct proper rites for the bird. In later years, the shrine fell into disrepair. During the {{nihongo|Daidō era|大同}}, {{nihongo|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|坂上田村麻呂}} restored the shrine. From the {{nihongo|Kenin|建仁}} to {{nihongo|Tenshō|天正}} eras (1201–1592), the {{nihongo|Nanbu clan|南部氏}} supported the shrine, followed by the {{nihongo|Tsugaru clan|津軽氏}} during the Edo period. In the Meiji era, the shrine was elevated to {{nihongo|Gōsha|郷社}} in 1873 and to {{nihongo|Ken-sha|県社}} in 1876. In 1907, the shrine absorbed the nearby {{nihongo|Inari Shrine|稲荷神社}} and {{nihongo|Kaijin Shrine|海津見神社}}. In December 2020, the shrine collaborated with the [[Virtual YouTuber]] {{nihongo|Aomori Rinko|青森りんこ}} to offer custom-designed {{nihongo|Ema|絵馬}} (wooden prayer plaques).{{Cite web|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ}} (Utō bird) * The [[Noh]] play ''{{nihongo|Utō|善知鳥}}'' * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功}}, a notable printmaker and painter who spent his childhood near the shrine. == External Links == * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Utō Shrine Official Website] (in Japanese) * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Utō Shrine] - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association (in Japanese) * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog] (in Japanese) {{Shinto shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utō Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Aomori City]] [[Category:Religious buildings in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Munakata Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]]
