{{出典の明記|date=2020年8月}} {{神社 |名称 = 善知鳥神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = [[青森県]][[青森市]]安方2-7-18 |位置 = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 宗像三女神 |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=善知鳥神社|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''善知鳥神社'''（うとうじんじゃ）は、[[青森県]][[青森市]]安方にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == 海の神、航海安全の神として知られる市杵島姫命・多岐津姫命・多紀理姫命の[[宗像三女神]]を主祭神として祀る。他に祭神として倉稲魂命、宮比命、猿田彦命、海津見大神が祀られている。 == 概要 == [[允恭天皇]]の時代に[[善知鳥安方|善知鳥中納言安方]]という者が勅勘を受けて外ヶ浜に蟄居していた時に高倉明神の霊夢に感じて干潟に小さな祠を建設し、宗像三神を祀ったのが神社の起こりと伝わる。安方が亡くなると、見慣れない一番の鳥が小祠のほとりに飛んできて雄はウトウと鳴き、雌はヤスカタと鳴くので、人々はこの鳥を安方の化身として恐れ敬ったが、ある日猟師が誤ってこの雄鳥を狙撃してしまい、以後雄鳥によって田畑が荒らされた。狙撃した猟師も変死したため、祟りを恐れた同郷の人々は雄鳥を丁重に弔った。 その後の流れは不明であるが、[[坂上田村麻呂]]の時代には同祠は荒廃していたため、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]年間に田村麻呂により社殿が造営されて再興された。[[建仁]]から[[天正]]期（[[1201年]]～[[1592年]]）の間は同地を支配した領主の[[南部氏]]により厚く崇敬されて庇護され、南部氏の騒動で同地が[[江戸時代]]に[[津軽氏]]の支配地になっても、同氏により手厚い庇護を受けた。[[明治時代]]に入り、[[1873年]]に[[郷社]]、[[1876年]]に[[県社]]に昇格。[[1907年]]7月に[[稲荷神社]]、同年9月に[[海津見神社]]を合祀し、現在に至る。 [[2020年]]12月に[[バーチャルYouTuber]]の[[青森りんこ]]とコラボレーションした[[絵馬]]の授与を始めた{{Cite web|和書|title=コラボ絵馬 授与開始 善知鳥神社×「青森りんこ」|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=なびたび北東北|accessdate=2021-01-14|language=ja}}。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[ウトウ]]（善知鳥／海鳥） *[[謡曲]]『[[善知鳥]]』 *[[棟方志功]]　版画家・画家。幼少期をこの神社の近くで過ごした。 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat}} *[http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ 善知鳥神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm 善知鳥神社]（青森県神社庁） *[https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ 公式ブログ 参拝のすゝめ] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うとうしんしや}} [[Category:青森県の神社]] [[Category:青森県の旧県社]] [[Category:青森市の歴史]] [[Category:青森市の建築物]]

{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Uto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |alternate_name = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyo|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬|1=Ema (Shinto)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|善知鳥神社|version=97953749|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Utou Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] [[Category:Utō Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Utou Shrine]] [[en:Utou Shrine]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]] [[sn:tou Shrine]]

{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Uto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |alternate_name = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyo|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬|1=Ema (Shinto)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|善知鳥神社|version=97953749|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Utou Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] [[Category:Utō Shrine]] }} [[sn:Utou Shrine]] [[en:Utou Shrine]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]] [[sn:tou Shrine]]

Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qq=}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daido era|qq=}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu clan|qq=}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru clan|qq=}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|qq=}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬|1=Ema (Shinto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ema|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema (Shinto)|qq=}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Virtual YouTuber|qq=}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rhinoceros auklet|qq=}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utou|qq=}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikō Munakata|qq=}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|善知鳥神社|version=97953749|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Utou Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] [[Category:Utō Shrine]] }} [[sn:Utou Shrine]] [[en:Utou Shrine]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]] [[sn:tou Shrine]]

{{Unreferenceds|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Uto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori, Aomori|qq=}}, Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |alternate_name = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Aomori, Aomori|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori, Aomori|qq=}}, Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyo|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ingyo|qq=}}, a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto Yasukata|qq=}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qq=}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daido era|qq=}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu clan|qq=}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru clan|qq=}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|qq=}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬|1=Ema (Shinto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ema|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema (Shinto)|qq=}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Virtual YouTuber|qq=}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rhinoceros auklet|qq=}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utou|qq=}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikō Munakata|qq=}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|善知鳥神社|version=97953749|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Utou Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] [[Category:Utō Shrine]] }} [[sn:Utou Shrine]] [[en:Utou Shrine]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]] [[sn:tou Shrine]]

Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ingyo|ja|允恭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ingyo|qq=}}, a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto Yasukata|qq=}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qq=}}, the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the {{ill|Daido era|ja|大同|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daido era|qq=}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu clan|qq=}} during their rule and continued to receive support from the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru clan|qq=}} after their rise in the Edo period. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|qq=}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering {{ill|ema|ja|絵馬|1=Ema (Shinto)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ema|13=User:Immanuelle/Ema (Shinto)|qq=}} in collaboration with the {{ill|Virtual YouTuber|ja|バーチャルYouTuber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Virtual YouTuber|qq=}} {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Rhinoceros auklet|ja|ウトウ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rhinoceros auklet|qq=}} (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utou|qq=}} (Noh play) * {{ill|Shikō Munakata|ja|棟方志功|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikō Munakata|qq=}} – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|善知鳥神社|version=97953749|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Utou Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] [[Category:Utō Shrine]] }} [[sn:Utou Shrine]] [[en:Utou Shrine]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]] [[sn:tou Shrine]]

{{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Uto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Aomori|Aomori, Aomori]], Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |alternate_name = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Aomori|Aomori, Aomori]], Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyo]], a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto Yasukata|qq=}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daido era]]. The shrine was later patronized by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] during their rule and continued to receive support from the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] after their rise in the Edo period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|qq=}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] in collaboration with the [[:en:VTuber|Virtual YouTuber]] {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Rhinoceros auklet|Rhinoceros auklet]] (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utou|qq=}} (Noh play) * [[:en:Shikō Munakata|Shikō Munakata]] – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|善知鳥神社|version=97953749|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] }} [[sn:Utou Shrine]] [[en:Utou Shrine]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]] [[sn:tou Shrine]]

Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daido era]]. The shrine was later patronized by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] during their rule and continued to receive support from the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] after their rise in the Edo period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|qq=}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] in collaboration with the [[:en:VTuber|Virtual YouTuber]] {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Rhinoceros auklet|Rhinoceros auklet]] (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utou|qq=}} (Noh play) * [[:en:Shikō Munakata|Shikō Munakata]] – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|善知鳥神社|version=97953749|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] }} [[sn:Utou Shrine]] [[en:Utou Shrine]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]] [[sn:tou Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Aomori (city)]] [[Category:History of Aomori, Aomori]]

During the time of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daido era]]. The shrine was later patronized by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] during their rule and continued to receive support from the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] after their rise in the Edo period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] in collaboration with the [[:en:VTuber|Virtual YouTuber]] {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Rhinoceros auklet|Rhinoceros auklet]] (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utou|qq=}} (Noh play) * [[:en:Shikō Munakata|Shikō Munakata]] – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|善知鳥神社|version=97953749|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Aomori City]] }} [[sn:Utou Shrine]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]] [[sn:tou Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Aomori (city)]] [[Category:History of Aomori, Aomori]] {{Commons category|Utou-jinja}}

{{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Uto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Aomori|Aomori, Aomori]], Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |alternate_name = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Aomori|Aomori, Aomori]], Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyo]], a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto Yasukata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daido era]]. The shrine was later patronized by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] during their rule and continued to receive support from the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] after their rise in the Edo period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] in collaboration with the [[:en:VTuber|Virtual YouTuber]] {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Rhinoceros auklet|Rhinoceros auklet]] (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utou|qq=}} (Noh play) * [[:en:Shikō Munakata|Shikō Munakata]] – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|善知鳥神社|version=97953749|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aomori City]] }} [[sn:Utou Shrine]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]] [[sn:tou Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Aomori (city)]] [[Category:History of Aomori, Aomori]] {{Commons category|Utou-jinja}}

In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1876. In December 2020, the shrine began offering [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] in collaboration with the [[:en:VTuber|Virtual YouTuber]] {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Rhinoceros auklet|Rhinoceros auklet]] (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utou|qq=}} (Noh play) * [[:en:Shikō Munakata|Shikō Munakata]] – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|善知鳥神社|version=97953749|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Utou Shrine]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]] [[sn:tou Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Aomori (city)]] [[Category:History of Aomori, Aomori]] {{Commons category|Utou-jinja}}

{{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Uto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Cerorhinca_monocerata.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Aomori|Aomori, Aomori]], Yasukata 2-7-18, Aomori Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E|scale:25000_region:JP-02_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Shinmei-zukuri |alternate_name = |Main Festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Aomori|lat_deg=40|lat_min=49|lat_sec=36.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=44|lon_sec=32.8|label=Uto Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|40|49|36.1|N|140|44|32.8|E}}}} }} '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Aomori|Aomori, Aomori]], Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}. == Overview == According to legend, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyo]], a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto Yasukata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried. During the time of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daido era]]. The shrine was later patronized by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] during their rule and continued to receive support from the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] after their rise in the Edo period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] in collaboration with the [[:en:VTuber|Virtual YouTuber]] {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Rhinoceros auklet|Rhinoceros auklet]] (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utou|qq=}} (Noh play) * [[:en:Shikō Munakata|Shikō Munakata]] – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|善知鳥神社|version=97953749|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Utou Shrine]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]] [[sn:tou Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Aomori (city)]] [[Category:History of Aomori, Aomori]] {{Commons category|Utou-jinja}}

During the time of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daido era]]. The shrine was later patronized by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] during their rule and continued to receive support from the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] after their rise in the Edo period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in September. In December 2020, the shrine began offering [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] in collaboration with the [[:en:VTuber|Virtual YouTuber]] {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=}}.{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Rhinoceros auklet|Rhinoceros auklet]] (Uto/sea bird) * {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utou|qq=}} (Noh play) * [[:en:Shikō Munakata|Shikō Munakata]] – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine. == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://utojinja.sakura.ne.jp/ Official Website of Uto Shrine] * [http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/tousei/jinja/12.htm Uto Shrine - Aomori Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://utojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Blog: Guide to Worship] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|善知鳥神社|version=97953749|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Utou Shrine]] [[ja:善知鳥神社]] [[sn:tou Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Aomori (city)]] [[Category:History of Aomori, Aomori]] {{Commons category|Utou-jinja}}

During the time of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daido era]]. The shrine was later patronized by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] during their rule and continued to receive support from the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] after their rise in the Edo period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1876. '''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Aomori|Aomori, Aomori]], Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Deities ==

The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ|1=Tagitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932592}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}.

== Overview ==

According to legend, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyo]], a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto Yasukata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried.

During the time of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daido era]]. The shrine was later patronized by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] during their rule and continued to receive support from the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] after their rise in the Edo period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in September.

In December 2020, the shrine began offering [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] in collaboration with the [[:en:VTuber|Virtual YouTuber]] {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=|1=Aomori Rinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104591587}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}}</ref>

== Related Articles ==

* [[:en:Rhinoceros auklet|Rhinoceros auklet]] (Uto/sea bird)
* {{ill|Utou|ja|善知鳥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utou|qq=|1=Utō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418959}} (Noh play)
* [[:en:Shikō Munakata|Shikō Munakata]] – a printmaker and artist who spent his childhood near the shrine.

'''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Aomori|Aomori, Aomori]], Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Deities ==

The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ|1=Tagitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932592}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}.

== Overview ==

According to legend, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyo]], a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto Yasukata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried.

During the time of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daido era]]. The shrine was later patronized by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] during their rule and continued to receive support from the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] after their rise in the Edo period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in September.

In December 2020, the shrine began offering [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] in collaboration with the [[:en:VTuber|Virtual YouTuber]] {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=|1=Aomori Rinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104591587}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}}</ref>

'''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Aomori|Aomori, Aomori]], Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Deities ==

The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ|1=Tagitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932592}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}.

== Overview ==

According to legend, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyo]], a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto Yasukata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried.

During the time of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daido era]]. The shrine was later patronized by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] during their rule and continued to receive support from the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] after their rise in the Edo period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in September.

In December 2020, the shrine began offering [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] in collaboration with the [[:en:VTuber|Virtual YouTuber]] {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|qq=|1=Aomori Rinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104591587}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}}</ref>

In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in September.

In December 2020, the shrine began offering [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] in collaboration with the [[:en:VTuber|Virtual YouTuber]] {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|1=Aomori Rinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104591587}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}}</ref>

'''Uto Shrine''' (善知鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Aomori|Aomori, Aomori]], Yasukata, Aomori Prefecture. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Deities ==

The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, known as deities of the sea and maritime safety: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐津姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ|1=Tagitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932592}}, and {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多紀理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ|1=Takiribime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932870}}. Additional deities worshipped include {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja|宮比命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, and {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|海津見大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}.

== Overview ==

According to legend, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyo]], a courtier named {{ill|Uto Yasukata|ja|善知鳥安方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto Yasukata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was exiled to Sotogahama. Inspired by a divine dream of Takakura Myojin, he built a small shrine on the tidal flats and enshrined the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}. Upon Yasukata's death, an unfamiliar bird appeared near the shrine. The male bird called "Uto," while the female bird called "Yasukata." Locals revered these birds as Yasukata’s incarnations. However, when a hunter mistakenly shot the male bird, the fields were ravaged, and the hunter died mysteriously, believed to be a curse. The male bird was then respectfully buried.

During the time of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], the shrine had fallen into ruin but was rebuilt under his command during the [[:en:Datong (disambiguation)|Daido era]]. The shrine was later patronized by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] during their rule and continued to receive support from the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] after their rise in the Edo period. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1876. In 1907, as a part of the [[Shrine Consolidation Policy]] it incorporated the local [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] in July and {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine|ja|海津見神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in September.

In December 2020, the shrine began offering [[:en:Ema (Shinto)|Ema (Shinto)]] in collaboration with the [[:en:VTuber|Virtual YouTuber]] {{ill|Aomori Rinko|ja|青森りんこ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Rinko|1=Aomori Rinko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104591587}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Collaboration Ema Now Available: Uto Shrine × "Aomori Rinko"|url=https://www.navitabi.jp/article/3831|website=Navitabi North Tohoku|accessdate=2021-01-14}}</ref>
